# ArchLens

ArchLens is MCP architecture analysis for real Java systems, built on Spoon and backed by a queryable architecture graph.

It indexes Java workspaces, projects architecture facts into a TinkerGraph model, and exposes typed MCP tools for tracing entrypoints, runtime paths, data movement, and change impact to agents and engineers.

## What it does

- Discovers entrypoints: REST endpoints, JMS/Reactive messaging consumers and producers, schedulers, EJB methods, event observers, WebSocket/SSE/gRPC methods
- Traces runtime call paths from entrypoints through services, repositories, outbound clients, and async boundaries
- Follows request parameters and message payloads to persistence, messaging, HTTP outbound, event bus, file/object storage
- Stitches multi-step workflows across brokers, event buses, shared fields, and repository handoffs
- Queries architecture neighborhoods, paths, dependencies, and impact slices from a TinkerGraph model
- Exports Mermaid diagrams, LikeC4 text, Markdown architecture docs, graph JSON, and a self-contained HTML graph viewer

## How it works

1. Scan Maven, Gradle, Kotlin DSL, and plain Java roots
2. Build components, entrypoints, call edges, data-flow paths, and workflow links with Spoon
3. Project the model into TinkerGraph
4. Expose typed architecture queries and renderers over MCP stdio
5. Generate Mermaid, LikeC4, Markdown docs, JSON graph data, and a standalone graph viewer

## Tech stack

Java 21, Spoon, MCP stdio, TinkerGraph, Mermaid, LikeC4

## Links

- GitHub: https://github.com/derPate/spoon-mcp-server
- Install guide: https://github.com/derPate/spoon-mcp-server/blob/main/docs/INSTALL.md
